summary: Called for urgent regional coordination; IMST activation, surge support, cross-border surveillance, laboratory, IPC, case management, RCCE, burial, logistics and mobilization priorities.
WHO Director-General determined the DRC and Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak to be a PHEIC.
Declared the ongoing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a Public Health Emergency of Continental Security.
Activated EU Health Task Force and deployed experts to support Ebola outbreak response.
Reported teams operating in northeast DRC, with emphasis on lack of approved Bundibugyo-specific vaccine and treatment and operational response challenges.
Stated close monitoring and highlighted lack of licensed vaccines/therapeutics; situates response in PHEIC/PHECS context.
Announced increased activities as outbreak intensified while assessing risk to the EU/EEA general population as very low.
Conveyed expert advisory recommendations on candidate treatments and vaccines for Bundibugyo virus disease.
Announced/was reported to allocate approximately USD 60 million to accelerate vaccine candidates by Moderna, IAVI and University of Oxford/SII.
